title: Do Not Start With Payment Infrastructure for South Asian Creators Until You Read This
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Ghana with a great idea, but you're stuck because you can't use PayPal or Stripe. Every day that passes means more missed sales and a dwindling bank account. You're not alone – thousands of creators worldwide face the same dilemma. But there's a specific mistake that's costing you money and time, and it's not the lack of a payment processor. It's the infrastructure you're building on top of it. If you start with the wrong foundation, you'll be setting yourself up for a world of trouble. The good news is that you can avoid this pitfall and build a store that can't be taken down. But you need to know what to look for.
The Mistake Almost Every Creator Makes First

You think that building a store is all about designing a beautiful website and listing your products. But the truth is, the backbone of your store is its payment infrastructure. If you start with a traditional payment processor, you're building on shaky ground. They can shut you down at any moment, without warning, due to 'terms of service violations' or 'geo-restrictions'. You'll be left with a store that's full of products but can't process payments. It's a nightmare scenario that's all too common in the digital creator community. Don't make the same mistake that so many others have made. Take a step back and reassess your payment strategy.
Why This Keeps Happening to Good Stores

The reason why so many creators fall into this trap is that they're relying on mainstream payment processors. These companies have strict terms of service and can shut down your account at any moment. They may not even inform you why your account has been terminated. This can happen due to a variety of reasons, including geo-restrictions, high-risk activities, or even a dispute over a single transaction. The result is that your store is brought to its knees, and you're left scrambling to find a new payment solution. It's a frustrating and time-consuming process that can leave you feeling defeated.
The Real Cost of Getting This Wrong

The cost of getting your payment infrastructure wrong can be staggering. Not only will you lose sales and revenue, but you'll also spend valuable time and resources trying to find a new solution. This can be a costly and time-consuming process, especially if you're relying on a traditional payment processor. Additionally, you may be faced with penalties and fines for violating their terms of service. The worst part is that you may never fully recover from this setback, and your store may never regain its former momentum. The damage can be long-lasting and far-reaching.
Traditional payment processors are a ticking time bomb for digital creators.
How to Protect Yourself Before It Happens

The good news is that you can protect yourself from this common mistake. The key is to build your store on a foundation that's secure, reliable, and flexible. This means using a payment infrastructure that's blockchain-based and decentralized. With this approach, you'll be able to process payments without relying on traditional payment processors. You'll also be able to access a global market and avoid geo-restrictions. This is the key to building a store that can't be taken down.
